commit.message:
Fix bot 409 conflict: retry with backoff instead of crashing

When Telegram returns 409 (another getUpdates request), wait
5-30s and retry instead of crashing the process, which caused
Docker to instantly restart and repeat the conflict loop.

commit.message:
Restrict guest registration (claim spots) to event organizer only (#122)

* Restrict guest registration (claim spots) to event organizer only

Previously any registered participant could claim empty slots for guests.
Now only the event's organizer can claim spots, enforced both in the UI
(canClaimSpot check) and at the database level (RLS policy).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add unit tests for claim spot restriction

Extract canUserClaimSpot helper to src/lib/utils.ts for testability.
Add tests verifying only organizers can claim spots, and EmptySlot
component renders/hides the Claim button based on the prop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix claimed spot showing organizer name and remove redundant Withdraw button

- Display the stored participant name for claimed spots instead of always
  overriding with the organizer's auth name
- Remove the "Withdraw" button from claimed spots since the trash/delete
  icon button already handles removal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove redundant claim number suffix from participant names

The +N badge already shows the claim number, so the "- 1" suffix in
names like "Test User - 1" is redundant. Now claimed spots just use
the entered name (or organizer's name as fallback). Badge number is
computed from position among claimed spots instead of parsed from name.

* Add integration test for Claim button visibility by organizer status

remaining" toast after eligible deletion |

mock for auth module |

## Test plan

count
- [ ] Delete a pending/processing job → see "Credit refunded" toast
- [ ] Delete a completed job → see "Job deleted" toast (no refund

commit.message:
Add RHOAI CVE analysis AgentSkill

Implement a Claude Code skill that automates CVE triage for Red Hat OpenShift AI
releases. The skill guides an agent through collecting CVE data from the Pyxis
container catalog, fetching detailed vulnerability info from the Red Hat Security
Data API, analyzing actual impact on OpenShift AI workloads, and generating
remediation reports.

Scripts (PEP 723, uv-runnable):
- fetch_rhoai_cves.py: vendored from rhoai-security-manifest, JSON-only output
- fetch_cve_details.py: queries Red Hat Security Data API with rate limiting
- generate_report.py: assembles markdown report from manifest + analysis JSON

Reference docs:
- CVE_ANALYSIS_METHODOLOGY.md: decision tree for CVSS/package/statement analysis
- REPORT_FORMAT.md: report structure spec and analysis JSON schema
- RED_HAT_SECURITY_API.md: API endpoint and response field reference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix owner /[handle]/ask showing curator AI instead of visitor AI

The owner path wrapped in CuratorProvider without VisitorProvider, so
useCurator() returned curator data (My AI messages). Now ALL logged-in
users get the same layout: CuratorProvider (shell) + VisitorProvider
(content). useCurator() prefers VisitorContext, so ChatView with
variant="visitor" gets visitor chat data — owners see exactly what
visitors see.

Simplified layout to two paths: logged-in (shell + both providers)
and anonymous (VisitorProvider only). Removed ownership check from
layout since VisitorContext handles isOwner detection internally.

commit.message:
Fix 413 error: direct-to-Supabase upload via signed URLs

Vercel serverless functions have a hard 4.5MB body limit, causing
413 errors on image/PDF uploads. Redesigned the upload flow:

1. Client sends metadata to /api/context/upload (action: "sign")
2. Server returns a signed Supabase upload URL
3. Client uploads file directly to Supabase (no size limit)
4. Client confirms via /api/context/upload (action: "confirm")
5. Server records the file in context_files table

The file never passes through Vercel, so 10MB uploads work fine.

commit.message:
fix: decouple embed gradient clipping from weight grad_clip (ALB-071)

C-EMBED-GRAD-001 (activation gradient clipping at GPU→CPU boundary) was
gated behind `if let Some(max_norm) = max_grad_norm`. When ALB-067
disabled grad_clip to work around the CPU-side norm bottleneck, it also
disabled embed clipping. Without it, 24-layer backward amplifies
activation gradients to ~1e35, overflowing CPU AdamW's second moment
buffer → NaN embedding weights → loss=0.0 by step ~100.

Five Whys:
1. Loss=0.0 at step 169 → all seqs produce NaN loss → valid_count=0
2. NaN loss → NaN embedding weights → NaN logits → NaN softmax
3. NaN embeddings → CPU AdamW overflow from unclipped activation grads
4. Unclipped activation grads → C-EMBED-GRAD-001 inside max_grad_norm gate
5. max_grad_norm=None → ALB-067 disabled grad_clip in YAML config

Fix: Always clip embed activation gradient with unwrap_or(1.0) default.
Also: always compute LM head grad norm for observability (R-004) even
when weight clipping is disabled, enabling proper ZClip monitoring.

Refs #133

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Dedup 66 duplicate pairs (within 3u) from approved nodes
- Accept c26 user corrections: 68 pending nodes promoted to approved,
  149 false positives removed (fog + duplicates + wrong positions)
- 16 user re-added nodes that v7 wrongly cleaned as fog are preserved
- Edge bounds from actual approved range, column/row artifact filter
- Reserved user negative slots (150) to prevent scan negs from dominating
- Lower thresholds (NCC 0.73, margin 0.02) for better recall
- 25 new candidates found (food 10, gold 12, stone 2, wood 1)
- Total: 4786 nodes (4761 approved + 25 pending)

commit.message:
fix: outcome evaluation was never firing (empty observations bug)

- Move evaluate_outcomes() AFTER market fetch in perception_node — was called
  before fetch, so state["observations"] was always empty, and no predictions
  could ever match a current price. 9 predictions are now ready for evaluation.
- Add TRADING_ENABLED short-circuit: skip draft/review/risk_gate when trading
  disabled — saves ~288 LLM calls/day and eliminates Telegram spam.
- Build lab/data_readiness.py + 14 tests (Loop failed overnight, we deliver)

commit.message:
feat: Add water_heater platform as alternative to climate (#116) (#120)

* feat: Add water_heater platform as alternative to climate (#116)

Adds an optional ESPHome water_heater platform for the balboa_spa
component, exposing the spa as a Home Assistant Water Heater entity.

The three supported modes map directly to Balboa spa state:
- ECO         → rest_mode=1 (sleep/rest, energy-saving)
- HEAT_PUMP   → rest_mode=0, highrange=0 (ready, standard range)
- PERFORMANCE → rest_mode=0, highrange=1 (ready, high temp range)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: update VERSION to 2.21 [skip ci]

* fix: Resolve compilation errors in water_heater component

- Remove duplicate cg.register_component call (register_water_heater
  already handles it, causing double-registration error)
- Remove redundant Component base class (WaterHeater already inherits
  from Component, causing diamond ambiguity)
- Use trailing-underscore member names (target_temperature_,
  current_temperature_, mode_) matching WaterHeater internals
- Fix call.get_target_temperature() usage (returns float, not optional)
- Implement required make_call() pure virtual returning
  WaterHeaterCallInternal(this)

All three configs now compile: ESP32/Arduino, ESP32/IDF, ESP8266

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: Simplify water_heater modes to OFF/ECO/PERFORMANCE and fix device_id unavailability

Map rest_mode/highrange to three intuitive modes: OFF (rest), ECO (ready/standard),
PERFORMANCE (ready/high-range). Add setup() override to restore saved state and
publish immediately so the entity is available in HA even before the first UART packet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix build

---------

Clean up combined plot: 2-panel layout, no WLS trend

Replace dual-axis single panel with two stacked panels sharing the x-axis:
- Top (accuracy): scatter/line (1 run) or median+IQR band (multi-run),
  overall mean dotted reference, no WLS trend
- Bottom (distances): both median lines (orange = any train, green = same-
  class train) with IQR shading on a single hop-distance axis, red dashed
  model-depth line

Separating the two y-scales into their own panels removes the dual-axis
confusion while keeping the trends visually aligned for comparison.

docs: soften fast-playwright-mcp to optional, upstream is sufficient

--snapshot-mode none on upstream @playwright/mcp provides the same 70-80%
token savings with Microsoft backing. fast-playwright-mcp's extras (per-tool
control, batch execution) are marginal gains (~2-3K tokens) with supply
chain risk from a 31-star single-maintainer fork.

commit.message:
fix(arb): move validation loop to standalone daemon thread — no event loop

run_in_executor futures still need the event loop to poll completion,
which hangs when the loop is CPU-starved. Solution: run the entire
validation cycle in a plain daemon thread using time.sleep() and
urllib.request. Zero event loop dependency — attribute assignments
on self.pairs are GIL-atomic.
